blocks.text: glanced over when Julian laughed. She looked back at the fire before he noticed her looking.
watched Cade take the story against himself with complete ease. Most people who do that have been practicing it a long time.
looked away before she could notice. The laugh had been real. It had been a good sound.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: How long have you all known each other? Because you talk like people who've been through something.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Ten years. First semester. I decided we were all going to be friends before any of them had agreed to it. Cade in particular took about three months to accept that the decision had been made.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: I came around.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: He did. Eventually.

blocks.speaker: Wren Cole
blocks.content: It worked.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: See. Wren gets it.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: The lake house three summers ago. Do you remember the kayaking afternoon?

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: We are not still on that.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: You went in circles for forty minutes, Dean. I was watching you from the dock. I didn't say anything at first because I didn't want to embarrass you. Then you hit the dock and I thought, he's had enough dignity preserved.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: I grazed the dock. And the paddle was clearly unbalanced — I checked it before I went in.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: You borrowed my paddle. The paddle was fine.

blocks.speaker: Cade Mercer
blocks.content: He hit the dock twice.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: Once. I grazed it once on the way in and once—

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: You apologized to the dock.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: I was being polite.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: What are you talking about? What dock?

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: It was three years ago and we have all agreed to—

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Dean's kayaking technique.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: Oh no.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: I'm being sacrificed again. This is fine. I accept this.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: How long were you actually in circles?

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: That's not—

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Forty minutes. We have video from the dock.

blocks.speaker: Dean Ashby
blocks.content: That video is inadmissible.
Sophie reached for Dean's arm while she was laughing. He was already turning toward her before she touched him.

blocks.speaker: Sophie Laurent
blocks.content: You okay, Nadia? You got quiet.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Of course. I was just going to say — the fire's getting low.
The fire was not getting low.

blocks.prompt: Something just happened at the edge of a good moment. What did you catch?

options.label: Nadia went somewhere else the moment Sophie reached for Dean. She came back fast — but she went.
The fire comment. A change of subject dressed up as an observation.
Nothing. It was a good night and not everything is something.
